zh.lifeSummary: 當John de Bradford於大約1210年出生在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋時，他的父親Sir Alexander de Bradford38歲，母親Alice35歲。他於大約1245年與Elena在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1265年享年56歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bradford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the many places, large and small, called Bradford; in particular the city in Yorkshire, which originally rose to prosperity as a wool town. There are others in Derbyshire, Devon, Dorset, Greater Manchester, Norfolk, Somerset, Cheshire, Wiltshire and elsewhere. They are all named with Old English brād ‘broad’ + ford ‘ford’.

History: This name was brought independently to North American by many different bearers from the 17th century onward. William Bradford (1590–1657), born in Austerfield in South Yorkshire, England, the son of a yeoman farmer, was among the Pilgrim Fathers who emigrated to North America on the Mayflower in 1620. He was a signer of the Mayflower Compact and in 1621 he was elected governor of Plymouth colony, being re-elected thirty times. Another William Bradford (1663–1752), printer, came from Barnwell, Leicestershire, England, to Philadelphia, PA, in 1685, subsequently moving to New York, where he set up a printing press and founded a paper mill. His grandson, also called William Bradford (1721–91), was known as ‘the patriot printer’, famous for his Philadelphia newspaper, which among other things denounced the Stamp Act, "which no American can mention without abhorrence".

variants.name: Braford
meanings: English: variant of   Bradford  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Brafford
meanings: English: variant of   Bradford  . This surname has now died out in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When John de Bradford was born about 1210, in Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Alexander de Bradford, was 38 and his mother, Alice, was 35. He married Elena about 1245, in Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1265, at the age of 56.

ko.lifeSummary: John de Bradford(이)가 대략 1210년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 뉴카스틀 업 타이니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Alexander de Bradford(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice(은)는 35세였다. 그는 대략 1245년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 뉴카스틀 업 타이니에서 Elena(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1265년 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de Bradford родился около 1210 г. в местности нюкастл упон тин, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Alexander de Bradford было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Elena около 1245 г. в местности нюкастл упон тин, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1265 г. в возрасте 56 лет.
